notes: FROZEN / daemon=incapable. Asserts the CORRECT unblock-time behavior, NOT the current (broken) one: parent ready->working at spawn, a kiro child session appears with ParentSessionID=parent.session_id, parent held working across the child's lifetime, parent working->ready after the child reports back. This is unobservable today — the kiro-cli adapter sets no ParentSessionID (parser.go), the flat ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl layout misses deriveParentSession's path-pattern, the JSONL has no {type:session,parentSession} header, and the parent ref kiro persists ('Record parent session ID in persisted subagent sessions') lives in a sidecar/.kiro/.subagents shape no irrlicht path reads. known_failing=true: a recording WILL fail these phases until a kiro parent-link adapter rule lands AND the foreground child's transcript lands under a tailed root. Observations are JSONL-derived only — kiro's <uuid>.jsonl carries no model/token/cost (those live in the <uuid>.json sidecar the parser does not read), so model=unknown, cost/tokens not asserted non-zero.

text: Parent kiro session appears in ready
User prompt drives parent ready -> working (spec bullet 1)
Subagent session materializes (UNBLOCK-TIME: under a tailed root) — a new SessionState for the child
Detector emits parent_linked binding child.session_id -> parent.session_id (spec bullet 2: ParentSessionID = parent.session_id) — UNBLOCK-TIME, requires a kiro adapter parent-link rule

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while the synchronous child is alive (spec bullet 4)
After the child reports back, the parent's turn ends: working -> ready (spec bullet 5)
